---
title: "Regulation and Remedies"
---

## Learning goals
- Understand regulatory tools (rate-of-return, price-cap, access pricing) and their economic underpinnings.
- Evaluate behavioral vs. structural remedies and monitoring.
- Apply industry-specific insights (energy, airlines, telecom).

## Core topics
- Access pricing (ECPR-style sketches), interconnection, unbundling.
- Incentive regulation: benchmarking, yardstick competition.
- Remedy design: structural divestitures, conduct restrictions, monitors, reporting.
- Evaluating remedy effectiveness with retrospective data.

::: {.callout-note title="Method box"}
- Benchmarking models and simple productivity comparisons.
- Simulation of remedy scenarios using cost/pass-through assumptions.
- Retrospective diff-in-diff on post-remedy outcomes.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Qualitative evidence"}
- Implementation plans, compliance monitoring reports.
- Regulator-stakeholder hearings; public comment synthesis.
- Operational feasibility assessments from engineering/ops teams.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Citations and comparative note"}
- Cite sectoral regulators’ rules and precedent (e.g., FCC, FERC, Ofgem, Ofcom, ACER) and classic regulation texts.
- Include remedy orders/monitor reports as sources; note differences in US vs. EU/UK approaches to behavioral vs. structural remedies.
- Reference benchmarking/yardstick cases (e.g., energy networks) where available.
:::
